methods.description: The diatom frustules were purified from raw diatomite to obtain the ultrapure biosilica microflakes following several steps.

steps.description: First, the as-received diatomite powders (Aldrich) were immersed and stirred overnight in nitric acid (2 mol l À 1 ) and sulphuric acid (1 mol l À 1 ) to remove the organic and metal impurities, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically treat raw diatomite powder with nitric acid and sulfuric acid to remove impurities.

steps.description: After the recycled filtration and the deionized water cleaning, the biosilica microflakes with different particle sizes were separated from the filtrated diatomite using recycled sedimentation processes in acetone.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the treated diatomite, clean with deionized water, and then separate microflakes by size using sedimentation in acetone.

methods.method_name: Growth of Hierarchical Biomorphic Graphene on Diatomite
methods.description: Process for growing hierarchical biomorphic graphene layers on diatomite templates using chemical vapor deposition (CVD) and subsequent template removal.

steps.description: Diatomite powders were put into a three-zone tube furnace.
steps.searchable_summary: Load purified diatomite powder into a tube furnace for CVD.

steps.description: The tube was pumped to vacuum and rinsed with 300 standard-state cubic centimetre per minute (sccm) of 10 vol% hydrogen-mixed argon gas to remove air before it was heated to 1,000°C within 40 min at ambient pressure.
steps.searchable_summary: Evacuate the furnace tube and purge with H2/Ar gas mixture.

steps.description: before it was heated to 1,000°C within 40 min at ambient pressure.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat the furnace to 1000°C.

steps.description: The samples were then held at this temperature for 100 min under a constant mixed gas flow of H 2 /Ar carrier gas (300 sccm) and methane (2 sccm) for graphene growth.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform graphene growth at 1000°C under flow of H2/Ar and methane.
outputs: Diatomite with graphene grown on surface

steps.description: After the furnace was cooled to room temperature, light grey powder was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: Cool the furnace to room temperature.

steps.description: The powder was then immersed in a hydrogen fluoride (HF) etching solution (molar ratio of HF: H 2 O: EtOH is 6.7: 27.8: 5.1) at room temperature (or a 6 mol l À 1 NaOH solution at 80°C) overnight to remove the biosilica templates.
steps.searchable_summary: Etch the biosilica template from the graphene-coated powder using HF or NaOH solution.

steps.description: After complete rinsing with water and ethanol, the graphene powder was obtained by freeze-drying at À 90°C and reduced pressure (B1 Pa) for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse the etched graphene powder with water and ethanol.

steps.description: by freeze-drying at À 90°C and reduced pressure (B1 Pa) for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze-dry the rinsed graphene powder.

methods.method_name: Preparation of Flexible Graphene Films (Meyer Rod Coating)
methods.description: Process for preparing flexible graphene films using an EC-stabilized graphene dispersion and Meyer rod coating on a mica substrate.

steps.description: An EC-stabilized graphene dispersion was obtained by adding the as-grown graphene powder into ethanol/terpineol solution containing EC (48% ethoxyl, Aldrich) according to Hersam's method 52 .
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare EC-stabilized graphene dispersion in ethanol/terpineol.

steps.description: A 0.001 g ml À 1 solution of EC in ethanol was first mixed with the graphene powders.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix EC solution in ethanol with graphene powder.
outputs: Mixture of graphene and EC solution

steps.description: After separated by filtration, the graphene/EC sediment was redispersed in ethanol/ terpineol solution with volume ratio of 9: 1 by shaking up to form a stable graphene suspension with different concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the mixture, collect the sediment, and redisperse in ethanol/terpineol (9:1 v/v) by shaking.
inputs: Mixture of graphene and EC solution

steps.description: One millilitre of the graphene dispersions was dropped on the edge of a mica substrate (50 mm in thickness).
steps.searchable_summary: Deposit graphene dispersion onto a mica substrate.

steps.description: A Meyer rod (18#, RD Specialties, USA) was pulled over the solution to leave a uniform wet film.
steps.searchable_summary: Coat a uniform wet film of the dispersion on the substrate using a Meyer rod.
outputs: Mica substrate with uniform wet film

steps.description: During the natural drying process, the graphene films were pressed under a 1 kg weight overnight to improve their formability.
steps.searchable_summary: Press the wet film under weight during drying.

steps.description: After drying, the graphene films were heated in Ar/H 2 atmosphere at 350°C for 60 min, and then rinsed with acetone and dried by blowing with nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: Anneal the dried graphene films in Ar/H2 atmosphere at 350°C.

steps.description: and then rinsed with acetone
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse the annealed graphene films with acetone.

steps.description: and dried by blowing with nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry the rinsed graphene films by blowing nitrogen gas.

methods.method_name: Preparation of Graphene Ink for Inkjet Printing
methods.description: Process for preparing graphene ink compatible with inkjet printing from graphene/EC dispersion by tailoring viscosity and surface tension.

steps.description: Prior to inkjet printing, the solution of graphene/EC in ethanol/terpineol was bath ultrasonicated for 60 min to yield small graphene fragments, and then filtered through a polycarbonate membrane with 1 mm pores (25 mm in diameter, Millipore).
steps.searchable_summary: Ultrasonicate graphene/EC dispersion to fragment graphene.
outputs: Ultrasonicated graphene dispersion with smaller fragments

steps.description: and then filtered through a polycarbonate membrane with 1 mm pores (25 mm in diameter, Millipore).
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the ultrasonicated dispersion through a 1 um polycarbonate membrane.

steps.description: The filtered solution was concentrated into graphene/EC terpineol dispersion by heating at 120°C to remove ethanol, yielding a graphene ink at a concentration of B0.2 w/v%.
steps.searchable_summary: Concentrate the filtered dispersion by heating at 120°C to remove ethanol.

steps.description: For successful inkjet printing, the graphene terpineol dispersion was diluted with ethanol at different volume ratios ranged from 1:8 to 1:2 to tailor the viscosity and surface tension.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute the concentrated ink with ethanol at various ratios to adjust viscosity and surface tension.
outputs: Graphene ink with tailored properties for inkjet printing

methods.method_name: Preparation of Graphene Films by Vacuum Filtration and Transfer Printing
methods.description: Process for preparing graphene films using vacuum filtration followed by roll-to-roll transfer printing onto a PET substrate.

steps.description: A volume of 200 ml of graphene ethanol dispersion with a concentration of 40 mg ml À 1 was vacuumfiltrated using a polycarbonate membrane with 0.45 mm pores (47 mm in diameter, Millipore).
steps.searchable_summary: Vacuum filter graphene ethanol dispersion using a 0.45 um polycarbonate membrane to form a film.

steps.description: The naturally dried graphene film was then pressed on the ethylenevinyl acetate-coated PET substrate with the graphene side in contact with the substrate at ca. 120°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer the dried graphene film onto an EVA-coated PET substrate using heat and pressure.
outputs: Graphene film transferred onto PET substrate

steps.searchable_summary: Perform scanning electron microscopy (SEM).

steps.description: ultraviolet-vis spectroscopy (Perkin Elmer Lambda 950) ... were conducted to characterize the materials, respectively.

steps.description: and static contact-angle measurements (OCA-20 contact angle system) were conducted to characterize the materials, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform static contact angle measurements.

methods.method_name: Hall Bar Device Fabrication and Measurement
methods.description: Fabrication and electrical measurement of Hall bar devices using graphene transparent electrodes on mica substrates.

steps.description: Standard photolithography was used to define the selected region and fabricate arrays of Hall bar devices.
steps.searchable_summary: Pattern Hall bar geometry on graphene using standard photolithography.

steps.description: Plasma etching (50 W, 60 s) was used to remove the unwanted regions.
steps.searchable_summary: Etch exposed graphene areas using plasma etching.

steps.description: Thermal evaporation (UNIVEX 300, Leybold Vacuum) was performed to deposit the metal electrodes with chromium/gold (5 nm/100 nm) as the contacts, which were found to form ohmic contact through annealing at 350°C in Ar/H 2 atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: Deposit metal contacts (Cr/Au) using thermal evaporation.

steps.description: which were found to form ohmic contact through annealing at 350°C in Ar/H 2 atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: Anneal the devices with metal contacts at 350°C in Ar/H2 atmosphere to form ohmic contacts.

steps.description: Four-probe electrical measurements were carried out in a Micromanipulator 6200 probe station with a Keithley 4200 semiconductor characterization system.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform four-probe electrical measurements on the Hall bar devices.

methods.method_name: Sandwich-Type Solar Cell Assembly and Measurement
methods.description: Assembly and photocurrent-voltage (J-V) measurement of a sandwich-type dye-sensitized solar cell using a PET-supported graphene film as the counter electrode.

steps.description: A sandwich-type solar cell was assembled by placing a PET-supported graphene film (counter electrode) on a N719 dye (Solaronix)-sensitized TiO 2 photoelectrode on FTO glass (working electrode, effective area of 0.3 Â 0.3 cm 2 ), and clipped together as an open cell for measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble the solar cell by stacking the graphene counter electrode and the dye-sensitized TiO2 working electrode and clipping them together.
inputs: N719 dye-sensitized TiO2 photoelectrode on FTO glass (working electrode)

steps.description: The cell was then filled with a liquid electrolyte composed of 0.1 M anhydrous LiClO 4 , 0.12 M I 2 , 1.0 M DMPII and 0.5 M tert-butylpyridine in dehydrated acetonitrile by capillary force.
steps.searchable_summary: Fill the assembled solar cell with liquid electrolyte using capillary force.
outputs: Assembled solar cell filled with electrolyte

steps.description: The photocurrent-voltage (J-V) measurements were taken using a digital source meter (Keithley 2,400) and a solar simulator (Newport, Oriel class A, SP91160A, USA) under AM 1.5 G spectra with a light power density of 100 mW cm À 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: Perform photocurrent-voltage (J-V) measurements on the solar cell under simulated AM 1.5 G sunlight.
